2606.06350.title: EDIT: Evidence-Diagnosed Intervention Training for Rule-Faithful LLM Grading
2606.06350.paper_summary: Reliable rubric grading requires more than accurate score prediction. Each judgement must be grounded in the mark scheme and evidence from the student answer. Existing credit-assignment and intervention methods, primarily designed for self-contained reasoning tasks such as mathematics reasoning, struggle in this setting because they do not identify where grading reasoning goes wrong or how the model's belief about the final mark changes during reasoning. We propose Evidence-Diagnosed Intervention Training (EDIT), a two-phase framework for training more rubric-faithful LLM graders. First, EDIT-SFT locates problematic reasoning steps using internal model signals: posterior belief over the final mark and input-grounding scores. It then revises only these local steps with help from a rubric checklist. Second, EDIT-RL calibrates the grader with belief-guided reward shaping, penalising large harmful belief drifts while still allowing helpful exploration. Experiments on two real-world, multi-subject grading benchmarks demonstrate that EDIT consistently outperforms strong supervised fine-tuning and reinforcement learning baselines on both in-domain and out-of-domain splits, with ablation studies confirming that internal-state diagnostics drive these gains.
2606.06350.paper_summary_zh: 可靠的評分標準需要的不僅僅是準確的分數預測。每一個判斷都必須基於評分方案和學生答案中的證據。現有的信用分配和干預方法，主要是為了自我包含的推理任務（例如數學推理）而設計，在這種情境中表現不佳，因為它們無法識別評分推理出錯的地方或模型對最終分數的信念在推理過程中如何改變。我們提出了證據診斷干預訓練（EDIT），這是一個兩階段的框架，用於訓練更符合評分標準的大型語言模型評分者。首先，EDIT-SFT 使用內部模型信號定位問題推理步驟：對最終分數的後驗信念和輸入基礎分數。然後，它僅修正這些局部步驟，並借助評分標準檢查表。其次，EDIT-RL 通過信念引導的獎勵塑造來校準評分者，懲罰大的有害信念漂移，同時仍然允許有益的探索。在兩個現實世界的多科目評分基準上的實驗表明，EDIT 在領域內和領域外的拆分中，始終優於強大的監督微調和強化學習基線，消融研究確認內部狀態診斷驅動了這些增益。
2606.06350.author: Zhihao Wu et.al.
2606.06350.authors: Zhihao Wu, Linhai Zhang, Taiyi Wang, Runcong Zhao, Peter Andrews, Cesare Aloisi, Yulan He
